query: Neapolitan-style pizza Margherita with tomato, mozzarella, basil, and high-heat home oven method

draft.title: Neapolitan Pizza
draft.description: Classic Neapolitan-style pizza Margherita with tomato, mozzarella, basil, baked in a high-heat home oven for an authentic taste.

ingredients.name: fresh mozzarella
ingredients.note: preferably mozzarella di bufala or fior di latte

steps.text: Dissolve fresh yeast in lukewarm water in a large bowl.
Add flour and salt, mix until combined, then knead on a floured surface for 10 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Add olive oil and knead briefly to incorporate.
Place dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with a damp cloth, and let rise for 1-2 hours until doubled in size.
Preheat your oven to the highest setting (250-280°C) with a pizza stone or inverted baking tray inside for at least 30 minutes.
Divide dough into 4 equal balls and let rest for 15 minutes.
Prepare the sauce by crushing canned peeled tomatoes by hand or with a fork; season lightly with salt.
On a floured surface, stretch each dough ball into a 25-30 cm round base, leaving a slightly thicker edge.
Spread a thin layer of tomato sauce over the base, tear mozzarella into pieces and distribute evenly, then add basil leaves.
Transfer pizza onto the hot stone or tray and bake for 7-10 minutes until crust is golden and cheese melted.
Remove from oven, drizzle with a little olive oil if desired, slice, and serve immediately.

warnings: Use a pizza stone or inverted baking tray preheated for best crust results.
Mozzarella can release water; drain excess moisture to avoid soggy pizza.
